text: When designing a REST API, there are several important best practices to follow. First, create clear and logical endpoints that represent resources, such as `/users` or `/products`. Use standard HTTP status codes to communicate results; for example, `200` for success, `404` for not found, and `500` for server errors. Always include comprehensive documentation that explains how to use the API, including examples of requests and responses. Additionally, implement versioning in your API URLs, like `/v1/users`, so that changes do not break existing integrations for users. These practices help ensure your API is reliable and easy to work with.
